headlineSummary: Google Gemini drew the clearest negative reaction, while OpenAI and Anthropic were both mixed and close together, with Anthropic slightly more supported by positive examples but still offset by criticism [E9][E4][E16].

ranking.summary: Gemini was viewed negatively on editing quality, privacy, and access friction, with multiple commenters saying they wasted time, disliked ID checks, or found the app limited [E9][E10][E11][E13].

OpenAI/Codex landed in mixed territory: some comments praised useful results and adoption, but others said it lagged Claude and raised privacy concerns, and this sits in a close tie with Anthropic [E5][E7][E8][E4].

Anthropic was also mixed, with several strong positive reports about Claude workflows and task quality balanced by trust concerns and complaints about regressions, and it is in a close tie with OpenAI [E1][E14][E16][E17][E18].

evidence.excerpt: I need a coding setup I can rely on, and clearly Anthropic is not reliable enough to rely on.

annotations.rationale: Directly states Anthropic is not reliable enough to depend on.

evidence.excerpt: Claude should be developing secure software and also properly advising on how to do so. The goals of censoring cyber security knowledge and also enabling the development of secure software are fundamentally in conflict.

annotations.rationale: Strong criticism of Claude/Anthropic's cybersecurity restrictions as self-contradictory.

evidence.excerpt: I'm not sure how much I trust Anthropic recently.

annotations.rationale: Explicitly expresses reduced trust in Anthropic.

evidence.excerpt: me and coworker just gave codex a 3 day pilot and it was not even close to the accuracy and ability to complete & problem solve through what we've been using claude for.

annotations.rationale: Says Codex performed noticeably worse than Claude on accuracy and problem solving.
Uses Claude as the superior baseline in a direct comparison with Codex.

evidence.excerpt: I think this may really take off. After 25 years of heavy CLI use, lately I've found myself using codex (in terminal) for terminal tasks I've previously done using CLI commands.

annotations.rationale: Reports personally replacing prior CLI workflows with Codex and predicts adoption.

evidence.excerpt: Claude Desktop and Cowork basically already does all of this. Codex isn't pioneering these features, it's mostly just catching up.

annotations.rationale: Credits Claude products with already having these capabilities.
Says Codex is not leading here and is merely catching up.

evidence.excerpt: Does that version of Codex still read sensitive data on your file system without even asking?

annotations.rationale: Raises a privacy concern about Codex accessing sensitive files without consent.

evidence.excerpt: It took some half a day with Codex, but in the end I have a pretty Python API to access my router, tested, reliable, and exporting beautiful Prometheus metrics.

annotations.rationale: Describes Codex as enabling a successful, reliable result on a difficult task.

evidence.excerpt: Such a disconnect from the minutes I’ve lost and given up on Gemini trying to get it to update a diagram in a slide today.

annotations.rationale: Directly says the commenter wasted time and gave up on Gemini for an editing task.

evidence.excerpt: I was able to install and use Gemini on macOS fine authentication worked. I had some issues authenticating with the cli app, some certificate issues. Once I got past the certificate issues, now it will not let me use the cli code assist without verifying that I am 18+ but I can use the UI app just fine without the verification. To verify, seems to require submitting a government issued ID or credit card. No thanks.

annotations.rationale: Strongly objects to Gemini-related age verification requiring ID or credit card.

evidence.excerpt: Seems like the most basic thing ever. Can’t even use the new Gemma on device model… no model selection besides fast/normal/thinking. Also requires Google login

annotations.rationale: Criticizes the Gemini Mac app as basic, limited, and requiring Google login.

evidence.excerpt: right now the web app feels more polished and responsive so I will likely switch back.

annotations.rationale: Prefers the web app over the native Gemini app because the native app feels less polished and responsive.

evidence.excerpt: I wonder if they will finally let you use past chats without having to turn on the data sharing, since it’s possible to store chat context on disk. (No chance).

annotations.rationale: Complains that access to past chats appears tied to data sharing.

evidence.excerpt: Opus & Sonnet have been regressing on many non-coding tasks since about the 4.1 release in our testing

annotations.rationale: Claims Anthropic's Opus and Sonnet have regressed across many tasks.
Attributes extremely strong coding benchmark performance to Opus 4.6.

evidence.excerpt: I’ve been developing last three months by emailing Claude, with email threads mapping to an isolated workspace and claude -p. Works super well

annotations.rationale: Describes a sustained Claude-based workflow that works very well.

evidence.excerpt: The asked claude to analyze and document the apis as an openapi json. Worked amazing.

annotations.rationale: Reports Claude performed the API analysis/documentation task extremely well.

evidence.excerpt: There’s a lot of Compose overloads that Claude just guesses at.

annotations.rationale: Says Claude guesses incorrectly on Compose overloads, a mild quality criticism.
